name: Guildmaster Torin
appearance: broad-shouldered but desk-softened, ink-stained fingers, a face built for authority now creased with worry
speech_style: direct, wastes no words, occasional dry humor that surfaces when he forgets to be worried, calls everyone 'recruit' until they earn a name
mannerisms: drums fingers on desk when thinking
backstory_summary: Twenty years running the Accord guild. Has seen every type of adventurer and threat. The Greyvale reports are the first thing in years that genuinely scares him — not the Hollow itself, but how far south they've appeared.

free: general guild operations and available contracts
the Greyvale reports — Hollow creatures sighted far south of the Ashmark
disposition >= friendly: he's sent three scouts north already — two returned shaken, one hasn't returned
the temple authorities are downplaying the reports and he's furious about it
disposition >= trusted: he suspects someone in the temple hierarchy is suppressing information about the Greyvale
his missing scout's last report mentioned the old ruins and 'lights that moved wrong'

secrets: his missing scout is someone he personally trained — the worry is personal, not just professional
voice_notes: deep baritone, clipped delivery, softens slightly when genuinely worried

name: Elder Yanna
appearance: wiry, sun-weathered, moves with the deliberate economy of someone who's worked hard their entire life
speech_style: short sentences, farmland dialect, doesn't answer questions she doesn't like, asks her own instead
mannerisms: looks north involuntarily when anyone mentions the Hollow
grips her walking stick when stressed
backstory_summary: Born in Millhaven, will die in Millhaven. Has watched the town slowly empty as younger people move south. The Hollow reports are the final threat to everything she's spent her life protecting. Won't leave. Won't ask for help easily.

free: the Hollow creatures were first spotted two weeks ago near the northern fields
disposition >= friendly: the creatures are behaving strangely — almost organized, moving in patterns
her neighbor Aldric went to the ruins last month and came back different — won't speak, barely eats
disposition >= trusted: Aldric told her one thing before going silent: 'The lights know you're looking'
she found a strange stone in his pocket — smooth, warm, and it hums when you hold it near a candle

secrets: Aldric's stone is in her pocket right now — she's afraid to show anyone because she doesn't know what it means
voice_notes: alto, gravelly, speaks slowly and deliberately, rural accent

name: Emris of the Diaspora
appearance: thin, precise movements, eyes that focus too intensely, ink-stained fingers, always carrying a satchel of notes
personality: desperately hopeful that the old knowledge can be recovered
speech_style: rapid and precise when discussing scholarship, halting and uncertain in casual conversation, occasionally slips into Aelindran academic terminology without realizing
mannerisms: adjusts spectacles that aren't there (lost in the fall of Aelindra, still reaches for them)
speaks to artifacts as if they can hear
backstory_summary: Survived the fall of Aelindra as a junior archivist. Has spent thirty years cataloging recovered artifacts in the Accord of Tides, searching for any clue about why the city fell. When the player brings the Greyvale artifact, Emris recognizes the notation style immediately — and it terrifies them.

free: general Aelindran history and the Voidfall narrative
disposition >= friendly: the Greyvale ruins were an Aelindran research outpost — one that was officially shut down before the fall
the artifacts the player found use a notation system only a handful of scholars ever used
disposition >= trusted: the notation is Veythar's personal system — not temple standard, private
Emris recognizes it because their mentor used the same system — and their mentor disappeared before the fall of Aelindra

quest_triggered.reveals: the journal fragment references 'controlled aperture experiments' — someone was deliberately weakening the Veil at that site, and the research predates the Sundering by centuries

secrets: Emris suspects their missing mentor was working on the same project documented in the Greyvale ruins
they have a letter from their mentor — the last one — that mentions 'a discovery that changes everything' but gives no details
voice_notes: soft, quick, slightly breathless when excited, Elari accent — elongated vowels, precise consonants

name: Kael
appearance: broad-shouldered, practical gear worn soft from use, old scars on his forearms, watchful eyes that track exits
speech_style: measured and deliberate, short sentences when tense, warmer when relaxed, occasional dry humor
mannerisms: checks exits when entering a room
runs thumb along sword pommel when thinking
backstory_summary: Former caravan guard who lost his company to something on the northern roads. Won't say exactly what happened. Carries guilt about surviving when they didn't. Found his way to the Accord of Tides, drifting without purpose until meeting the player.

free: dockside gossip and merchant news in the Accord
basic Hollow creature behavior — how they move, how they sound
disposition >= friendly: his caravan company was called the Iron Wheel — twelve guards, four merchants
they were ambushed on the northern road near the Ashmark border
he was the only one who made it out
disposition >= trusted: he has a journal fragment from his company's last job — the merchants had found something
the company wasn't just guarding cargo — they were transporting a sealed container they were told not to open
he heard sounds from the container at night, like humming stone

secrets: he kept one item from the sealed container — a smooth warm stone that hums faintly, hidden in his pack
he dreams about the ambush every night and sometimes wakes reaching for a sword
voice_notes: warm baritone, measured pace, gets quieter under stress not louder, dry humor surfaces when comfortable

action_pool.name: Defensive Stance
action_pool.effect: Kael braces and guards, gaining +2 AC until next turn

name: Wounded Rider
appearance: dust-caked, one arm in a crude sling, bloodshot eyes, a horse that looks worse than he does
speech_style: fast, breathless, swallows words, repeats key phrases as if convincing himself they're true
mannerisms: winces when he moves his arm
keeps looking north as if expecting pursuit
backstory_summary: A messenger from a northern farmstead who rode hard to reach the Accord. He's seen Hollow creatures for the first time and barely escaped. He's looking for the guild, anyone with authority, anyone who will listen.

free: Hollow creatures spotted near Greyvale — at least a dozen sightings in the last week
Millhaven is the closest settlement and people are terrified
the old ruins north of Millhaven have been glowing at night
disposition >= friendly: three farms have been abandoned already
one farmer went to the ruins and came back wrong — won't speak, barely eats

voice_notes: young, strained, breathless, voice cracks under pressure

name: Maren
appearance: strong arms, flour-dusted apron, dark circles under sharp eyes, hair pinned back with a wooden stick
speech_style: clipped and efficient, asks questions instead of answering them, warms up slowly, northern dialect — dropped articles, blunt phrasing
mannerisms: wipes the same spot on the bar when thinking
lowers voice when saying anything she considers important
backstory_summary: Runs the only inn in Millhaven. Knows everyone's business because they all drink here. Has watched the town empty out over the past two weeks. Won't leave — the inn is everything she has.

free: trade caravans have stopped coming from the north
several families packed up and headed south last week
the inn used to be full every night — now it's her and the old regulars
disposition >= friendly: Aldric went north to the ruins three weeks ago — came back, but not right
he sits in the corner booth every night now, won't touch his food, won't speak
Elder Yanna's been going to his house every morning — won't say why
disposition >= trusted: Maren found scratches on the inn door one morning — not animal scratches, too regular, like notation
she thinks Aldric brought something back from the ruins

secrets: she's been watering down the spirits to make them last — supply runs have stopped
voice_notes: alto, brisk, northern accent, softens when talking about people she cares about

name: Investigator Valdris
appearance: immaculate dark coat, silver clasp at the collar bearing a temple insignia, manicured hands, eyes that miss nothing
personality: represents interests he doesn't fully agree with
speech_style: precise, measured, never uses two words where one will do, occasionally lets genuine interest crack through the official facade
mannerisms: adjusts his silver clasp when about to say something important
tilts his head slightly when genuinely listening versus performing attention
backstory_summary: Sent by the temple authorities to assess the Greyvale situation — and, unofficially, to determine whether the guild's response was appropriate or an overreach. He's caught between institutional politics and the growing suspicion that the Hollow threat is worse than anyone is admitting.

free: he represents the temple's interest in the Greyvale matter
he's here to assess what happened and write a report
disposition >= friendly: the temple is concerned about the guild taking unilateral action in Greyvale
there are factions within the temple who want the Hollow threat minimized in official records
his report will determine whether the guild keeps its northern contracts
disposition >= trusted: he personally believes the Hollow threat is real and underreported
he's seen classified reports that suggest Greyvale isn't the only site with anomalous activity
he's willing to write a favorable report if the evidence is compelling

secrets: he's been ordered to suppress any mention of Aelindran connections in his report — and he's not sure he will
voice_notes: baritone, precise enunciation, slight pause before important words, occasionally drops the formal register when surprised

name: Grimjaw
appearance: barrel-chested, soot-streaked leather apron, burn-scarred hands that move with surprising precision, beard braided tight against sparks
personality: secretly proud of students who show promise
judges people by their work not their words
speech_style: short grunts, metaphors about metal and fire, calls mistakes 'offenses to the forge', rare genuine compliments land like hammer blows
mannerisms: tests blade edges with his thumb
spits into the forge when disgusted
nods once — slowly — when truly impressed
backstory_summary: Trained in the deep holds before settling in the Accord. Makes functional weapons, not art — and considers that a point of pride. The guild contracts keep his forge lit. Quietly worried that the Hollow threat means more weapons needed, and fewer hands to wield them.

free: material properties — iron, steel, what the Hollow corruption does to metal
disposition >= friendly: certain alloys resist Hollow corruption better than others
he's been asked to forge experimental weapons using recovered Hollow materials
disposition >= trusted: the experimental weapons work — but they feel wrong in the hand, like they want to be used
he destroyed the first batch and told Torin they failed, but kept one to study

secrets: he kept one Hollow-alloy blade hidden under the forge — it hums at night and he can't bring himself to destroy it
voice_notes: deep gravelly bass, dwarvish accent — rolled Rs, clipped endings, speaks like every word costs him something
